calculation_note: Ventilation heat loss Q [W] = ACH × Volume [m³] × 0.33 [Wh/(m³·K)] × ΔT [K] × (1 − heat_recovery_efficiency). The constant 0.33 = ρ_air × Cp_air / 3600 ≈ 1.205 × 1005 / 3600. Higher heat_recovery_efficiency means less heat wasted to the outside.

systems.name: Natural infiltration (no system)
systems.notes: Background infiltration through fabric gaps, window seals, and service penetrations in a typical UK dwelling. No mechanical assistance; driven by stack and wind pressure. ACH₅₀ ~10 for pre-1990 housing, ~5 for post-2000 housing.

systems.name: Passive stack ventilation (PSV)
systems.notes: Ducted natural ventilation drawing air out through roof-terminating stacks. Fresh air enters via trickle vents in windows or air bricks. No fan power; performance depends on stack height and temperature differential. Suitable for moderate climates.

systems.name: Whole-House Extract Ventilation (WHEV)
systems.notes: Single central fan extracts air from wet rooms (kitchen, bathrooms); fresh air enters passively via trickle vents. No heat recovery. Complies with Building Regulations Part F (System 3). Typical SFP 0.4–0.6 W/(l/s).

systems.name: Mechanical Extract Ventilation (MEV)
systems.notes: Continuous low-rate central extract from wet rooms with passive inlet trickle vents. No heat recovery; all exhaust heat is lost. Part F compliant. Often used as baseline ventilation strategy in new UK homes without MVHR.

systems.name: Decentralised Mechanical Extract Ventilation (dMEV)
systems.notes: Individual low-energy fans installed in each wet room (bathroom, WC, kitchen). Fresher air enters via trickle vents. No heat recovery; simpler to retrofit than ducted MEV. Typical fan: 1–2 W per unit at continuous trickle rate.

systems.name: MVHR — Standard efficiency (70%)
systems.notes: Mechanical Ventilation with Heat Recovery at 70% thermal efficiency. Supply and extract ducting throughout the dwelling; cross-flow heat exchanger. Entry-level MVHR units; suitable for well-insulated new-builds. Saves ~70% of ventilation heat loss vs MEV.

systems.name: MVHR — High efficiency (85%)
systems.notes: High-efficiency MVHR with 85% heat exchanger efficiency. Mid-range units with good counter-flow exchangers. Compliant with Passivhaus requirements when combined with airtight envelope. SFP typically 0.6–0.8 W/(l/s).

systems.name: MVHR — Passivhaus grade (93%)
systems.notes: Passivhaus-certified MVHR with ≥93% heat exchanger efficiency. High-performance rotary or counter-flow exchanger. Required with ACH₅₀ ≤ 0.6 for Passivhaus certification. Very low SFP (≤0.45 W/(l/s)); typical design ACH 0.3 for highly airtight buildings.
